hero.eyebrow: Augustinian Roman Catholic Parish in Bristol
hero.title: Prayer, welcome, and charity in the spirit of St Nicholas of Tolentino.
hero.welcome: A calm, dignified parish home for liturgy, pastoral care, and community life: rooted in the Eucharist, attentive to the neighbour, and open to all who seek God.

slides.alt: Interior of St Nicholas of Tolentino church.
Mass being celebrated at the altar.
Members of the parish community gathered near the church entrance.

quickMassIntro.title: Quick Mass Times
quickMassIntro.copy: Sunday worship, weekday liturgy, confession, and livestream information as published by the parish.

mission.title: A parish identity shaped by worship, welcome, and service
paragraphs: The visual language of this parish draws quietly on the story of St Nicholas of Tolentino: the star of hope, the lily of purity, bread for the poor, and Augustinian hospitality at the heart of parish life.

The parish's published mission joins worship with practical solidarity: support for refugees and asylum seekers, food and utility help for those in need, and partnerships with Borderlands, the Wild Goose Cafe, One25, and Alabare CCC.
bullets: Prayer, welcome, and Augustinian community life held together
Foodbank, refugee support, and accompaniment of people on the margins
Synod listening, safeguarding, and parish leadership made visible online
mission.imageAlt: Painting of St Nicholas of Tolentino holding a lily.
mission.caption: St Nicholas of Tolentino remains the spiritual thread running through parish worship and service.

parishLife.title: Community that looks outward
parishLife.copy: Parish groups, food support, refugee accompaniment, and shared worship all appear together in the parish's public life.

cards.title: Groups, leadership, and shared life
cards.body: Parish life includes the Parish Pastoral Council, Parish Finance Committee, Community Stewards, Community Elders, Servant Leaders, catechists, youth-led Mass ministry, and hosted Ethiopian Orthodox worship.
cards.imageAlt: Members of the parish gathered inside the church.

cards.title: St Nicholas Bread and Borderlands
cards.body: St Nicholas Bread opens Monday, Wednesday, and Friday, 10:00am to 12:45pm. Borderlands is based at the Assisi Centre and accompanies refugees, asylum seekers, and people facing exclusion.

cards.title: Moments of worship and community
cards.body: The parish gallery includes baptisms, first Eucharist, confirmation, the 170 Anniversary, and community celebration images.

contactHighlight.title: Come to church, speak to the parish office, or support the mission
contactHighlight.copy: Find the church, parish office, priest contacts, parking, safeguarding routes, and parish giving in one clear place.
